pullRequestBaseGuidance.normalMessage: PR targets the normal trunk branch. A merge enters the normal deployment path.

informationalBases.vibe: This PR targets an optional direct-deploy/demo branch. It is different from the normal main-to-release pipeline; confirm the user intentionally wants this alternate environment path.

promotionOnlyBases.dev: Dev is intended to receive releases from the normal deployment pipeline, not as a long-lived GitHub PR branch. Retarget to the normal branch unless the repository explicitly documents a dev branch.
promotionOnlyBases.development: Development is intended to receive releases from the normal deployment pipeline, not as a long-lived GitHub PR branch. Retarget to the normal branch unless the repository explicitly documents a development branch.
promotionOnlyBases.test: Test is intended as a deployment environment, not a normal GitHub PR branch. Retarget to the normal branch unless the repository explicitly documents a test branch.
promotionOnlyBases.testing: Testing is intended as a deployment environment, not a normal GitHub PR branch. Retarget to the normal branch unless the repository explicitly documents a testing branch.
promotionOnlyBases.stage: Stage is intended as a promotion target in the deployment pipeline, not a normal GitHub PR branch. Retarget to the normal branch and promote the release after validation.
promotionOnlyBases.staging: Staging is intended as a promotion target in the deployment pipeline, not a normal GitHub PR branch. Retarget to the normal branch and promote the release after validation.
promotionOnlyBases.prod: Prod is intended as a protected production promotion target, not a GitHub PR branch. Retarget to the normal branch and use the production approval/promotion path.
promotionOnlyBases.production: Production is intended as a protected production promotion target, not a GitHub PR branch. Retarget to the normal branch and use the production approval/promotion path.

pullRequestBaseGuidance.nonstandardMessage: PR targets a nonstandard base branch. Explain how this differs from the normal deployment path before proceeding.
